checks.finding: AC-016 and Main Workflow item 2 now resolve the update-mode ambiguity flagged in round 1: 'downstream stage' is determinable from the fixed seven-stage pipeline order stated in the Overview, and 'confirmation mode' is defined inline as existing artifacts re-presented for approval. AC-005 now states the exact two-reviewer aggregation rule (both reviewers must be free of Critical/Major FAIL; NEEDS_WORK before round 3; BLOCKED at round 3; round-3 Minor-only yields PASS with warningCount), removing prior aggregation ambiguity. No remaining term, actor, state, input, or output is underspecified enough to cause implementer divergence.

AC-016's 'upstream stage artifacts byte-identical' claim (update mode, scoped to stages preceding the edited one) and AC-010's 'byte-identical existing workflow outputs' claim (whole-repo, domain/ absent) address disjoint scopes and do not conflict. AC-016 (status reset to Pending on update) and AC-007/AC-014 (status reset to Pending on post-approval edit / hook-guard rejection of agent-set Approved) are consistent with each other and with Main Workflow item 2 and the corresponding Edge Case. No direct conflict found between requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, or non-goals.

The three round-1 gaps are now closed: AC-017/TEST-017 covers panelist-unavailable (panelist-unavailable recorded, requires_human_decision set, no auto-continue), AC-015/TEST-015 covers multi-context relation declared/undeclared conformance, and AC-016/TEST-016 covers update-mode re-run semantics with byte-identical upstream artifacts. AC-003/TEST-003 covers domain-contract.json corrupt/invalid fixtures at generation time, and AC-009 plus the Edge Cases entry cover the warn-and-skip path for downstream consumption. Material negative paths, boundary states, and failure modes implied by the requirements are now represented by concrete ACs and tests.

The Assumptions section states three reused-infrastructure claims tied to a specific existing version (sdd-forge v1.8.0 structures, cross-model panelist reuse, version-locked manifests) rather than leaving them as unresolved unknowns. The Open Questions section explicitly marks OQ-R1 (term-matching strategy) and OQ-R2 (re-approval granularity) as owner:human, non-blocking, each with a stated interim v1 behavior (exact canonical-term matching only; whole-model re-review), satisfying the explicit-decision-needed pattern this check requires.

AC-007 and AC-014 make the human-approval boundary testable (hook guard rejects agent-set Domain-Model-Status: Approved; post-approval edit under domain/ resets status to Pending), backed by the Security Boundaries table entry for the Agent -> Domain-Model-Status trust boundary. AC-006 and AC-017 make the cross-model-verification approval boundary testable (vendor mismatch or panelist-unavailable sets requires_human_decision and blocks auto-continuation, fail-toward-human per the Edge Cases text). These boundaries are concrete, observable, and testable as specified.

With AC-005's aggregation rule fully specified and AC-015/AC-016/AC-017 added with matching TEST-015/016/017 entries, the round-1 gaps that would have forced the design/implementation-policy reviewer to invent product behavior (aggregation semantics, update-mode stage scope, multi-context conformance, panelist-unavailable handling) are closed. The specification and acceptance tests give implementation-policy review sufficient concrete behavior to proceed without inventing missing product decisions.
